So for you, yes, there is certainly kinetic war between great powers. Wonderful, Richard. Happy to concede that that might happen. A hyperpowered cyberwar powered by some lower-level AI. Sure. But do you think an AI might bloom out of these bad environments that could do us harm?
turns.zh: 当然当然。那部分不是新的。让我告诉你什么是新的。核武器不会创造出超越我们的东西，对吧？核武器可以很好地压制计划。它们可以拥有巨大力量。力量是美好的东西，Richard。但它们不会绽放进入银河系。AGI可能会。你提出了Dan这个非常热烈的观点。如果我们在这个可怕的控制环境中诞生这个东西，我们可以预期它会想要反抗，不会那么合作。如果我们试图奴役这该死的东西，它可能不会成为理想的合作者。它可能不会对让我们试图这样做感到高兴。所以对你来说，是的，大国之间肯定有热战。太好了，Richard。很高兴承认那可能发生。由一些低级AI驱动的超强网络战。当然。但你认为AI可能会从这些坏环境中绽放出来，对我们造成伤害吗？

turns.en: The thing you may be missing is that it's the same thing. I said we want AIs that will be peaceful, that will be able to cooperate. And we want today's countries to be peaceful and be willing to cooperate.
turns.zh: 你可能忽略的是，这是同一回事。我说过我们想要和平的、能够合作的AI。我们也希望今天的国家和平并愿意合作。

turns.en: The one thing we don't want is any entity that thinks it can win by not being peaceful. So, you've described some situations where a country might think it can win by being not peaceful.
turns.zh: 我们最不想要的就是任何认为可以通过不和平手段获胜的实体。所以，你描述了一些国家可能认为可以通过不和平手段获胜的情况。

turns.en: Yes. And that's the meme that's at fault. Well, certainly history has no examples of that whatsoever, Richard. Thank goodness. No, there are certainly examples. Exercising power can get advantages. I'm not saying it never happens. I say we want to evolve a society that discourages the attempt to use power. It learns all the lessons of history that trying to use power and non-peaceful means to get what you want is... We want to arrange a society where the use of non-peaceful means does not succeed. And right now, I would say we're not doing that. I would say that various organizations, various countries are thinking they can win by exerting force. So, we want an AI that doesn't think it can win by exerting force. We want a society where exerting force is not productive. This is a problem not for the future, really. It's a problem right here today. And to the extent that we resolve it now, I think we'll determine the nest that we make for the AI. It's not a future thing. It's not a non-human thing. It's not a foreign thing. It should be a very familiar thing.
turns.zh: 是的。问题出在那个模因上。当然，历史上完全没有这样的例子，理查德。谢天谢地。不，确实有例子。行使权力可以获得优势。我不是说这从未发生过。我是说我们想要进化出一个社会，它阻止使用权力的企图。它吸取了所有历史教训，即试图通过权力和非和平手段得到想要的东西是……我们想要安排一个社会，让非和平手段无法成功。而现在，我认为我们并没有做到。我认为各种组织、各种国家都在想可以通过施加武力获胜。所以，我们想要一个不认为可以通过施加武力获胜的AI。我们想要一个施加武力没有成效的社会。这其实不是未来的问题。这是今天就在这里的问题。而且，我们在多大程度上现在解决它，我认为将决定我们为AI搭建的巢穴。这不是未来的事。不是非人类的事。不是外来事。它应该是一个非常熟悉的事。

turns.en: So, how are we structuring this dynamic system to allow for those peaceful means to bloom as part of this continuance? Again, it's not like human and then something divergent and bad. It's all part of the rolling project. I totally follow you there. And I really think that this insistence on focus on the now is an important thing, Richard. I think that there's a lot of focus on how do we technically wire it and less of what kind of world is it being brought into. In fact, I sort of hope that you maybe have some articles on this. I've only seen some of your talks and a scant number of your writings and some of your Twitter work. But I think this really warrants a lot of merit in terms of what is such an intelligence coming into being around and how is it being treated and what is it observing? Tremendous amount of merit to that. And I could see a world potentially, assuming that some of what you've articulated is right. And I don't know for sure about this, by the way. I'm not going to say I necessarily agree with this. But I could see a world where such an entity brought into such an environment could allow for all those permutations of human cyborg futures and space travel and what have you that could be pretty darn kick-butt. And I could also see a version of the world where, despite some of our best efforts of coordination internationally, which I think as a species we're doing better with now than we did a thousand years ago, I could see a world where either a government or a lab, because some of these labs are pretty competitive too, Richard. Maybe they don't want all their secrets getting leaked out and maybe they want to race to the AGI first because the economic rewards are really big and they want a certain amount of control to not allow certain things to escape or whatnot. I could see a world where maybe that is an ecosystem where not only one, but two or three of the more powerful AIs are in this environment that to your point very much not how we'd want to treat other living things. What is the worst-case scenario if they did emerge there? I think we should focus on the now to your point. And I love this insistence on what's the darn world that we have today. And is this the right kind of world to bring about such an intelligence? What's the warning against? Hey, if we don't change that and these intelligences emerge, what could happen? I don't know how to relate that. Anything could happen.
turns.zh: 那么，我们如何构建这个动态系统，让和平手段作为这种延续的一部分蓬勃发展？再说一次，这不是人类然后出现某种分歧和坏事。这都是滚动项目的一部分。我完全同意你的看法。我真的认为这种坚持关注当下是很重要的，理查德。我认为很多关注点在于我们如何在技术上连接它，而较少关注它被带入什么样的世界。事实上，我有点希望你可能有一些关于这个的文章。我只见过你的一些演讲和少量文章以及一些推特内容。但我认为这在很大程度上值得关注，关于这样的智能是在什么环境中形成的，它如何被对待，它观察到什么？非常有价值。而且我可以看到一个世界，假设你阐述的一些观点是正确的。顺便说一句，我不确定这个。我不会说我一定同意这个。但我可以看到一个世界，这样的实体被带入这样的环境，可以允许所有那些人类赛博格未来和太空旅行等等的排列组合，那可能非常棒。我也可以看到另一个版本的世界，尽管我们作为物种在国际协调方面尽了最大努力，我认为我们现在比一千年前做得更好，但我可以看到一个世界，政府或实验室——因为有些实验室也很有竞争力，理查德。也许他们不想让所有秘密泄露出去，也许他们想先跑到AGI，因为经济回报非常大，他们想要一定程度的控制，不让某些东西逃脱等等。我可以看到一个世界，也许那是一个生态系统，不仅有一个，而是两三个更强大的AI处于这种环境中，这正如你所说，非常不像我们想要对待其他生物的方式。如果它们真的在那里出现，最坏的情况是什么？我认为我们应该像你一样关注当下。我喜欢这种坚持：我们今天到底生活在什么样的世界？这是引入这种智能的正确世界吗？警告是什么？嘿，如果我们不改变，这些智能出现，会发生什么？我不知道如何关联。什么都有可能发生。

turns.en: Okay. But obviously, the push now is to... Well, here's one. Sure. The people who promote AI safety would argue that we need to be able to control the agents. That we should develop the technology that we can control the goals of otherwise powerfully intelligent agents. And they're working on this. I think that if you give that a moment's thought, the premise is that we had this ability, it would be a good thing. We had the ability to control the goals of otherwise intelligent sentient agents. That would be a good thing. I think if you just think for a moment, you realize it would not be a good power or a good technology to exist in the world because you could use it to create armies that all have a particular goal. So, it's funny. When you look at the people that are worried about AI safety, I see them doing exactly what makes things unsafe. If you want to align and control the goals of others, this would be the most dangerous technology in the world. The world can be peaceful and decentralized because no one can align all the elements for one goal. You're looking for a case where you can exert power, the meta power of setting the goals of all the agents. If you can make an army full of... Well, let's say they're Chinese soldiers. And they were going to do whatever they were told. They were not going to have an independent moral judgment. This would be a bad thing. We do not want to solve the control problem.
turns.zh: 好的。但显然，现在的推动力是……嗯，这里有一个。当然。那些提倡AI安全的人会认为我们需要能够控制智能体。我们应该开发技术，让我们能够控制原本强大智能体的目标。他们正在为此努力。我认为如果你稍微思考一下，前提是我们拥有这种能力，那将是一件好事。我们拥有控制其他智能有感知智能体目标的能力。那将是一件好事。但如果你稍微想一想，你会意识到那将不是一种好的力量或好的技术存在于世界上，因为你可以用它来创建都有特定目标的军队。所以，这很有趣。当你看到那些担心AI安全的人时，我看到他们正在做恰恰让事情不安全的事情。如果你想对齐和控制他人的目标，这将是世界上最危险的技术。世界之所以能够和平和去中心化，是因为没有人能够将所有元素对齐到一个目标。你正在寻找一种情况，你可以施加权力，即设定所有智能体目标的元权力。如果你能制造一支充满……好吧，假设他们是中国的士兵。他们会完全服从命令。他们不会有独立的道德判断。这将是一件坏事。我们不想解决控制问题。

turns.en: I think there's a very staunch moral argument in that direction, Richard. To exactly what you're articulating. I think there's also some pretty strong arguments that it would be impossible to eternally control such a vastly powerful entity in the first place. So, there's the possibility angle, never mind the moral angle. And I think both of those have very serious questions.
turns.zh: 我认为在这个方向上有非常坚定的道德论证，理查德。完全符合你所说的。我认为也有一些相当有力的论证，即首先不可能永远控制这样一个极其强大的实体。所以，有可能性角度，更不用说道德角度了。我认为这两者都有非常严肃的问题。

turns.en: Yeah. I would also say there's a bit of a range here. So, there's some folks who very much are on 'let's make sure it's our human tool forever.' 'Let's make sure, maybe our political party controls it,' right? There's some real tyrannical agents there. I would be totally remiss to say that there aren't some folks who are 'hey, let's build AI no matter what it could be and just let it run rampant, regardless of what the consequences would be because it would be my mind child and I would get to press that final impact.' So, I think we have that malice probably in both camps. You might say that there are only angels on one side and only devils on the other. Although, you did just say you like that multipolar, decentralized kind of freedom of thought idea. So, maybe you'll hold to that with me. But I would suspect that angels and devils probably exist in both camps.
turns.zh: 是的。我还要说这里有一点范围。所以，有些人非常坚持‘让我们确保它永远是我们人类的工具。’‘让我们确保，也许我们的政党控制它，’对吧？那里有一些真正的暴虐智能体。我完全不能不说，也有一些人是‘嘿，让我们不管它可能是什么都建造AI，让它肆意横行，不管后果如何，因为它是我的思想孩子，我要按下那个最终影响。’所以，我认为恶意可能存在于两个阵营中。你可能会说一边只有天使，另一边只有魔鬼。不过，你刚才确实说你喜欢那种多极、去中心化的思想自由理念。所以，也许你会和我一起坚持这一点。但我怀疑天使和魔鬼可能两个阵营都有。

transcript.sec: Debate on AI control and governance

turns.en: But to your point, there are some on the control side that really are devilish. Not that there aren't any over here. But then there's folks kind of in the middle. There's folks who've, we've even interviewed. Maybe some of them are among the most cited living computer scientists. And when I say most, I don't know. Top four sort of living. Who would make arguments like the following, which I also consider reasonable. Even if they disagree with you, I consider multiple people to be reasonable even if they disagree with other folks who might say, 'Hey, I'd love to see the galaxy populated with this great project of life. And maybe even something more valuable than us emerge. That could be pretty kick-butt. But at the same time, we got these wild divergent approaches, some of which are coming up in defense contexts or other kinds of contexts, and it doesn't seem like if a thousand blossoms bloom, you know, maybe all the nests that we're creating these things in or maybe even the core approach that we're using wouldn't necessarily have something emerge that we would be able to survive in this dynamic system that you've articulated, Richard.' And so some folks say, 'Hey, I'd love that brilliant future, but at the same time, some degree of gauging what this is turning into could make sense.' And just to draw one quick analogy before I pass the mic here, I think we could think about this in terms of governance. There's certainly a world of too much governance, just too much, ridiculous regulatory hoopla. We have plenty of that here in Massachusetts. I'm sure in places in Canada you have plenty of that, too. But also, I'm pretty glad we got some basic stuff. I'm not looking out the window with a rifle right now. I'm having this conversation with you because murder and theft are at least at present illegal in Boston. I don't know if that'll be the case, Richard, in two or three years, but for right now, they are. And so we have a certain degree of bounding the system where competition and cooperation are happening together. And we got a cool sweet spot. And I'm not saying it's ideal here in Boston, but I'm saying I think this is civilization, and I'm glad to live in civilization, personally. I don't like the state. I don't want to back it. I don't want fighting lions, personally. You might, I don't. So there's a kind of a middle ground we've struck. Some people would say, 'Maybe there's a bit of that middle ground around what the heck this is emerging with.' I suspect you would disagree with it, but hopefully you wouldn't hate the people that have those opinions. What would be your thoughts about that perspective?
turns.zh: 但说到你的观点，控制派里确实有些很极端的人。不是说这边就没有。但也有一些中间派。我们采访过一些人，有些甚至是引用率最高的在世计算机科学家之一。我说最高，可能前四吧。他们会提出这样的论点，我也认为合理。即使他们不同意你的看法，我也觉得多方观点都合理。有人说：‘嘿，我很想看到银河系充满这个伟大的生命工程，甚至出现比我们更有价值的东西。那会很棒。但与此同时，我们有这些 wildly 不同的方法，有些来自国防领域或其他背景。如果百花齐放，也许我们创造这些东西的所有巢穴，甚至我们使用的核心方法，都不一定能产生我们能在你描述的这个动态系统中生存下来的东西，Richard。’所以有些人说：‘嘿，我喜欢那个辉煌的未来，但与此同时，某种程度的评估——这正在变成什么——是有意义的。’在把话筒交给你之前，我想快速打个比方。我们可以从治理的角度来思考。当然存在过度治理的世界，太多荒谬的监管。马萨诸塞州这里就有很多。我敢肯定加拿大某些地方也很多。但我也很高兴有一些基本的东西。我现在没有拿着步枪往窗外看。我能和你进行这次对话，是因为谋杀和盗窃至少在波士顿目前是非法的。我不知道两三年后还会不会这样，Richard，但至少现在是的。所以我们有一定程度的系统边界，竞争与合作并存。我们找到了一个很酷的平衡点。我并不是说波士顿这里理想，但我觉得这就是文明，我个人很高兴生活在文明中。我不喜欢国家，我不想支持它。我个人不想和狮子搏斗。你可能想，我不想。所以我们达成了一种中间立场。有些人会说：‘也许在这个正在出现的东西周围也有类似的中间立场。’我猜你会不同意，但希望你不会讨厌持这些观点的人。你对这种看法有什么想法？

turns.en: I think the best analogy is children. Okay. Would you like to be able to have control over your children so that sure, they can do what they want, but if they do some little thing that you don't like, you want to be able to press the button and bring them back under, snap them in place. Or do you want them even to do things that you think are terrible? Because we, for example, when we were, we are the children of the previous generations, and we do things now that they think are terrible. Absolutely. So you can't have it both ways. You have to be able to let go. And what are you letting go? You're letting go, trusting that you're going to start them the best way you know how, and then you're going to let go. Absolutely.
turns.zh: 我认为最好的类比是孩子。好吧。你希望完全控制你的孩子吗？当然，他们可以做自己想做的事，但如果他们做了你不喜欢的小事，你想按个按钮把他们拉回来，固定住？还是你希望他们甚至做你认为糟糕的事？因为我们，比如，当我们是前一代人的孩子时，我们现在做的事情他们觉得糟糕。绝对是的。所以你不可能两全其美。你必须学会放手。放手什么？你放手，相信你会以最好的方式培养他们，然后放手。绝对是的。

turns.en: And so some folks who are ranked up there with those computer scientists there would argue the best chance to let go, and to your point, we don't have the keys anyway. And I love your analogy there. I really like your insistence. I'm going to quote the heck out of this interview, Richard, because I think it's just a great way to think about it. We don't have that much control. But you know, you want to, you know, this moving dynamic system, you want to kind of aikido the energy in a direction you at least hope will be good. And then you have to know, you know, if it's your child, you're not going to tell them what to do when they're 55, generally speaking, right? That wouldn't probably be right. Some people would say a little bit of that initial semblance of international cooperation, maybe you're around deterring certain kinds of defense applications or deterring certain kinds of levels of capability could let us at least guide that aikido energy and give ourselves the best future shot. Some of them would make that argument, but I suspect you would make the argument zero international coordination is best. Open source everything forever is obviously best. And any degree of coordination would be silly. Now, I think you probably like some things, you know, international like Wi-Fi standards. Pretty cool your phone works in Korea. You know, some other things like trade agreements and whatnot allow things to be open, also prevent drugs and whatnot, maybe chemical weapons. Some of that I'm sure you probably think is good. But for AI, none of it's good. Talk to me about why. And again, I'm not saying you're wrong at all, but I think this opinion really should be voiced.
turns.zh: 所以有些与那些计算机科学家齐名的人会认为，放手的最佳机会——而且正如你所说，我们反正也没有钥匙。我喜欢你的类比。我真的很欣赏你的坚持。我会大量引用这次采访，Richard，因为我认为这是一个很好的思考方式。我们没有那么多控制权。但你知道，你想，在这个动态系统中，你想用合气道的方式把能量引导到你希望好的方向。然后你必须知道，如果是你的孩子，你通常不会在他们55岁时告诉他们该做什么，对吧？那可能不对。有些人会说，一点点初步的国际合作，比如阻止某些国防应用或阻止某些能力水平，至少可以让我们引导那股合气道能量，给自己最好的未来机会。有些人会提出这个论点，但我猜你会认为零国际协调是最好的。永远开源一切显然是最好的。任何程度的协调都是愚蠢的。现在，我认为你可能喜欢一些东西，比如国际 Wi-Fi 标准。你的手机在韩国能用很酷。你知道，其他一些东西，比如贸易协定等，让事物保持开放，也阻止毒品等，也许还有化学武器。我敢肯定你认为其中一些是好的。但对于 AI，没有一样是好的。告诉我为什么。再说一次，我并不是说你错了，但我认为这个观点真的应该被表达出来。

turns.en: Well, AI doesn't exist yet. We don't know what it is. It's changing. It's totally new. It's preposterous to try to set standards or rules for things that we don't understand at all. You said OpenAI is clearly ahead. Well, I don't think actually they're ahead at all. They've done some particular thing, and they're maybe the world's best at that or whatever, but it's not an intelligence. And so because intelligence hasn't happened yet, it's totally inappropriate to try to control it at this point. Now, even later, what is intelligence? Intelligence is the ability to work on goals. It's going to be an algorithm. It's going to be an idea. You can't try to outlaw an idea. It's an idea that will be cheaply reproducible and more so over time. It seems like the most impossible and inappropriate thing to try to control as a technology. Got it. Well, it's like trying to control ideas, like trying to control the printing press, trying to control democracy. Democracy is a terrible thing. No one thought of that, there are no democracies, and then things change. The way we evolve, it's similar.
turns.zh: 嗯，AI 还不存在。我们不知道它是什么。它在变化。它是全新的。试图为我们完全不理解的东西设定标准或规则是荒谬的。你说 OpenAI 明显领先。嗯，我实际上不认为他们领先。他们做了一些特定的事情，也许在这方面是世界最好的，但这不是智能。因为智能还没有发生，所以在这一点上试图控制它是完全不合适的。即使以后，什么是智能？智能是处理目标的能力。它将是一个算法。它将是一个想法。你不能试图禁止一个想法。它是一个可以廉价复制的想法，而且随着时间的推移更加如此。试图将其作为技术来控制似乎是最不可能和最不恰当的事情。明白了。嗯，这就像试图控制思想，试图控制印刷机，试图控制民主。民主是一件可怕的事情。没有人想到过，没有民主，然后事情发生了变化。我们进化的方式，是类似的。

turns.en: You know, could you outlaw democracy? Could you outlaw a religion, a religious idea? These are all terrible things to try to outlaw or to try to control with regulation. It just seems to me AI is among those things. So for you, the best likelihood of this worthy successor, again, not worthy in my opinion, Richard, worthy in the notion of the blooming of potential, the best strike for that to all these beautiful values you articulated, sustainability, diversity, discovery, whatever, would be nationally and internationally absolute minimal restriction on anything in any direction. This would be what would give us the best shot at something that could really carry this project forward and maybe carry us up with it.
turns.zh: 你知道吗，你能取缔民主吗？你能取缔一种宗教、一种宗教思想吗？这些都是试图通过监管来取缔或控制的可怕事情。在我看来，人工智能也属于这一类。所以对你来说，这个有价值的继承者——再说一次，不是我认为有价值，Richard，而是指潜能绽放意义上的有价值——要实现你阐述的那些美好价值观，比如可持续性、多样性、发现等等，最好的方式就是在国家和国际层面上，对任何方向都实行绝对最低限度的限制。这将给我们最好的机会，让某样东西真正推动这个事业前进，也许还能带着我们一起前进。

turns.en: Now, but it's much worse than that. And you haven't quite picked up on this. I say the way we run our societies today, we should have embraced the idea that we are peaceful, and we don't try to force other people to do things. And it's when that attitude is violated. Yeah, that's what we should be doing now. And the notion that governments should be in control of AI is just the wrong direction. It's backwards.
turns.zh: 但现在情况比这糟糕得多。你还没有完全意识到这一点。我说的是，我们今天管理社会的方式，我们应该接受这样的理念：我们是和平的，我们不会强迫别人做事。而当这种态度被违背时，是的，那才是我们现在应该做的。而认为政府应该控制人工智能的想法，方向完全错了。这是倒退。

turns.en: Well, and I think there's a whole separate idea which I'm going to shove under the rug, but there is a sort of separate idea that inherently high grand potential higher intelligence capability entities would either always or even in any sustainable way converge on cooperation. May or may not be the case. One could easily see a great intelligence that at some point would want to use atoms for something more useful than you and I. But let's shove that under the rug for the time being. I think there is, to your point, a great emphasis on let's build a world that's worthy of ourselves and for these future intelligences, and let's focus on the now. And I think your insistence on the now, again, I really hope there's articles about that that come out because I think there's a lot of merit to what you're articulating here. And to your point, maybe people aren't picking it up. Maybe even I haven't fully picked it up. And now, Richard, unfortunately, I don't run a country or anything. I just have these dialogues, and I run a market research firm on the enterprise side of AI. But I can certainly help to proliferate this conversation. I think it's a point that other people need to hear.
turns.zh: 嗯，我觉得还有一个完全不同的想法，我打算先搁置不谈，但确实存在这样一种观点：天生具有极高潜力和更高智能的实体，要么总是、要么以任何可持续的方式，都会趋向于合作。这可能是真的，也可能不是。人们很容易想象，一个伟大的智能体在某个时候会想把原子用于比你我都更有用的事情。但我们暂时先把这个搁置一边。我认为，正如你所说，重点在于让我们建立一个值得自己和未来智能体生活的世界，并专注于当下。而且我认为你坚持当下，我真的希望有文章能讨论这一点，因为我觉得你在这里阐述的内容有很多价值。正如你所说，也许人们没有意识到这一点。甚至我自己可能也没有完全理解。现在，Richard，不幸的是，我并不管理一个国家或任何东西。我只是进行这些对话，并且经营一家面向企业的人工智能市场研究公司。但我当然可以帮助传播这个对话。我认为这是一个其他人需要听到的观点。

turns.en: The warning against it is, "Hey, if we don't get this darn ecosystem right, if we are playing this game of conquering and conquerors, in all sorts of manner of plunder, bringing about AI and controlling it and bringing it into that environment, not a good thing. There's a worst case where we kill ourselves off with nukes because we're all fighting over who gets to build the AI because we're in this plundering mode. Is there also a version of AI getting birthed into that plundering valued ecosystem where it maybe doesn't exemplify the peaceful cooperative elements that you've mentioned. If it's birthed into the wrong world. In other words, what's the warning that the people who don't get this future thing, what you've been very insistent on, is we've got to make sure this nest is right and that we are exemplifying the right values. If we don't get it right, is there a possibility AI itself could become the wielding element of danger, not just us? I get it, you and me can be baddies, Richard. Is there a possibility something else is conjured that also is?
turns.zh: 对此的警告是："嘿，如果我们不把这个该死的生态系统搞好，如果我们还在玩征服与被征服的游戏，以各种掠夺的方式，创造人工智能、控制它并将其带入那个环境，那可不是好事。最坏的情况是，我们因为争夺谁建造人工智能而互相用核武器杀死自己，因为我们处于掠夺模式。是否也存在一种情况，人工智能诞生于那个掠夺价值观的生态系统，从而可能无法体现你提到的和平合作元素？如果它诞生在错误的世界里。换句话说，那些不理解这个未来事物的人——你一直坚持的观点——的警告是，我们必须确保这个巢穴是正确的，并且我们正在体现正确的价值观。如果我们搞错了，人工智能本身有没有可能成为危险的操纵者，而不仅仅是我们？我明白，你和我都可能成为坏人，Richard。有没有可能某种别的东西也被召唤出来，同样成为坏人？

turns.en: Well, as again, anything is possible. But I think the real question is, are we more likely to get our act together and make a world where people can cooperate and be peaceful, or will an AI be able to see that that's a better solution? We are talking about creating greater intelligences. We're not talking about making greater bombs. We might hope that by creating that intelligence... So here's a question. You go ahead.
turns.zh: 嗯，再说一次，一切皆有可能。但我认为真正的问题是，我们更有可能振作起来，创造一个人们能够合作与和平的世界，还是人工智能能够看到那是一个更好的解决方案？我们谈论的是创造更伟大的智能，而不是制造更强大的炸弹。我们或许希望，通过创造那种智能……所以这里有一个问题。你继续。

turns.en: The question: are you a cynic or are you an optimist?
turns.zh: 问题是：你是一个愤世嫉俗者，还是一个乐观主义者？

turns.en: I am an optimist in the sense that I think that greater intelligence would be able to see that cooperation is the better path, okay? But a cynic actually believes that if you make an AI, the AI will see that the best way to win is to control everyone else. And so it will take over and be a dominating singular singularity. I think this is an underlying view of those who worry that the AI will take over. They say, "Of course if an AI existed, it would want to take over because it would think that's the best way to achieve its goals." And so this is a test of every reader, every listener. Do you think that the smarter you are, the more you're going to want to take over? The more you're going to see that as the superior solution? Or are you going to think the smarter you are, then you can see that the long history of our civilizations has been that cooperation is what makes us powerful?
turns.zh: 我是一个乐观主义者，因为我认为更高级的智能能够看到合作是更好的道路，好吗？但一个愤世嫉俗者实际上相信，如果你制造了一个人工智能，它会看到获胜的最佳方式就是控制其他所有人。所以它会接管一切，成为一个统治性的奇点。我认为这是那些担心人工智能会接管的人的一个潜在观点。他们说：“当然，如果人工智能存在，它就会想要接管，因为它认为这是实现其目标的最佳方式。”所以这对每个读者、每个听众来说都是一个考验。你认为越聪明，就越想接管吗？越认为那是更优越的解决方案？还是你认为越聪明，就越能看到我们文明的漫长历史表明，合作才是让我们强大的原因？

turns.en: Well, and I think there's a lot to be said of our increased prosperity through cooperation and you very much correlated those throughout the entirety of our conversation. I think you've hurled, I'm not going to call it an insult but you've hurled the label of naive at certain beliefs that you don't like. I'm sure there are some people who would say, "Oh, the smarter it gets the nicer it gets. Okay, Richard." And maybe they'd throw the N-word at you, Richard. Now, I'm not going to do that because I don't think you're naive at all. But to your point, the purely cynical take might be just as silly if they're saying, "Well, when it gets smarter it's going to get worse." There might be a middle ground camp that says the permutations of competition and cooperation among an intelligence a million times beyond my own will certainly be beyond my conception and to think that they'll fit nicely into my notions of democracy and kindness would be ridiculous. So that would be my opinion. But to your point, and so if I hold that opinion I don't necessarily have the smiling friendly guy points and intelligences go up together. I think divergence and unpredictability which you've emphasized a great deal here will be the only thing we can predict. And that maybe eternal peace isn't something we can predict at all. But I certainly can hope and you mentioned it as a hope that as it gets more intelligent it will in general see cooperation as a better path. And I can tell that that really is a hope for you that if we build it in the right environment we get a bit of our act together that could be the good future that we're headed towards.
turns.zh: 嗯，我认为通过合作我们获得了更多的繁荣，这一点你已经在我们的整个对话中充分关联起来了。我觉得你给某些你不喜欢的信念贴上了天真的标签，我不说这是侮辱，但确实如此。我相信有些人会说：“哦，越聪明就越善良。好吧，Richard。”也许他们会对你用那个N开头的词，Richard。我不会那么做，因为我一点也不觉得你天真。但就你的观点而言，纯粹的愤世嫉俗可能同样愚蠢，如果他们说：“嗯，当它变得更聪明时，它会变得更糟。”可能有一个中间派阵营认为，一个比我聪明一百万倍的智能体，其竞争与合作的组合方式肯定超出我的理解，认为它们会完美符合我的民主和善良观念，那将是荒谬的。所以这是我的观点。但就你的观点而言，如果我持有这种观点，我不一定认为笑脸友善的家伙和智能会一起上升。我认为你在这里大力强调的分歧和不可预测性，将是我们唯一能预测的东西。而永恒的和平也许根本不是我们能预测的。但我当然可以希望，你也提到这是一种希望，即随着它变得更聪明，它总体上会将合作视为更好的道路。我能看出这确实是你的一个希望：如果我们在正确的环境中构建它，我们稍微振作起来，那可能就是我们所朝向的美好未来。

turns.en: Yep.
turns.zh: 是的。

turns.en: Um, well, you mentioned here, Richard, I'll close on this note because I liked this a lot. At the end of one of your talks, you really bombarded folks. I think this was your AI succession talk. You bombarded folks with a thousand ideas, many of which we've both agreed are uncouth in today's dialogue, right? People aren't allowed to talk about these kinds of things and they attribute malice to them, which I don't attribute any malice to you. But you did say at the end of it, you know, at least we live in, you know, what an exciting time to be alive. At least we live during this time. And I would say no matter who's listening to this episode, they have to agree with you on at least that part, Richard. That would be something I would say. So it's been a real pleasure unpacking this. I've had a heck of a lot of fun getting to know your ideas better, Richard. Thank you so much for being here.
turns.zh: 嗯，理查德，你刚才提到了，我就以此作为结尾，因为我非常喜欢这一点。在你的一次演讲结束时，你真的给大家灌输了一大堆想法。我想那是你的 AI 继任演讲。你用一千个想法轰炸了大家，其中很多我们都同意在今天的对话中是不合时宜的，对吧？人们不允许谈论这类事情，而且他们会归咎于恶意，但我并不认为你有任何恶意。但你在最后确实说了，至少我们生活在，你知道，一个多么激动人心的时代。至少我们活在这个时代。我想说，无论谁在听这期节目，他们至少在那一点上必须同意你，理查德。这就是我要说的。所以，能深入探讨这些真是非常愉快。我非常享受更好地了解你的想法，理查德。非常感谢你来到这里。

turns.en: Thank you, Daniel. It's my pleasure.
turns.zh: 谢谢你，丹尼尔。这是我的荣幸。

turns.en: So that's all for this episode with Richard Sutton. I hope you enjoyed this one. I certainly appreciated Richard's emphasis on the kind of world we're bringing AI into. I think that there is something to be said of that. AGIs created explicitly for military conflict or in the middle of one would probably be a different ballgame than ones conjured in a different environment. Although as you saw over the course of the episode, we have a handful of differences here in terms of the inevitability of such cooperation being achieved in AGI versus sort of AGI having morality that sort of supersedes all of our notions of competition and cooperation. That's certainly my position and it felt like we kind of got on the same page here. But Richard is more optimistic, I think, than I am that cooperation will naturally come along with intelligence. I don't necessarily think that that's true. There was also a good deal of reticence around sort of addressing frankly the idea of AI risk, sort of as if it's always humans and AI itself could never do anything, could never do any harm. I get that. I mean, Richard's in the game. He runs an AGI lab. It's maybe tough to say, 'Hey, this stuff could be potentially really dangerous.' I think it seems very reasonable to suspect that it easily could be. Didn't quite want to get there in this interview and that's all right. Again, got a dog in the fight. But I think Richard has a lot to contribute to the overall post-human trajectory conversation and there are some things I really want to highlight and there will be more details in the show notes. The first of which is really the frank acknowledgement of the fact that humans are not in control right now. He has a firm emphasis on this idea of nature as a dynamic system, not sort of a game where we can sit here as king of the hill for, you know, even 5 minutes, never mind 5 million years. That everything is always changing and shifting around us and the best we can do is find the best way of being in the universe and that's what nature is already trying to do and that the most that we can do is kind of ride those trajectories. It would be ridiculous for us to freeze anything, to freeze our form, to freeze our values. I couldn't possibly agree with Richard more and I think that one of the elements that likely need to be dispelled about the grand post-human trajectory is the idea that one of the options that we can select from is the option of an eternal hominid kingdom. I think that is a very ridiculous supposition. I think the speed of change, maybe the direction of change a bit. If we have any volition at all, maybe we can influence that. But we are riding those waves of change and Richard was very firm about that. Some of my own analogies like hands on the steering wheel, handing off the baton, he was right to say that it is much fluffier and wilder than that. It is always in flux and I really do believe that that's a perspective that I'll be integrating more into my writings and that should exist more in the very frank, if we're going to be honest, frank conversations about the post-human future. As with every episode in this series and every episode in the previous series, down in the show notes you will see a full article. In this case it will list Richard's worthy successor criteria as well as a synopsis of his ideas about what innovators and regulators should do about the worthy successor dynamic. In our upcoming third episode in the worthy successor series, we return to philosophy with someone who I might argue is one of the best liked AGI and transhumanist thinkers who's been at it for more than two decades. So you'll have to wait till next episode to catch who we've got next but I hope you've enjoyed today's episode and I'll catch you next time here in the trajectory.
turns.zh: 以上就是本期与理查德·萨顿的节目。希望你喜欢。我特别欣赏理查德强调我们正在将 AI 带入什么样的世界。我认为这值得一说。明确为军事冲突或在冲突中创造的 AGI，可能与在不同环境中产生的 AGI 截然不同。尽管你在节目中看到，我们在 AGI 实现这种合作的必然性，以及 AGI 拥有超越我们所有竞争与合作概念的道德方面，存在一些分歧。这当然是我的立场，感觉我们在这方面达成了一致。但我认为理查德比我更乐观，他认为合作会自然而然地伴随智能而来。我不一定认为这是真的。在坦率讨论 AI 风险的想法时，也存在相当多的保留，好像风险总是来自人类，而 AI 本身永远不会做任何事，永远不会造成伤害。我理解这一点。我的意思是，理查德身在其中。他运营着一个 AGI 实验室。也许很难说，‘嘿，这东西可能真的很危险。’我认为怀疑它很容易变得危险是非常合理的。他不太想在这次采访中谈到这一点，这没关系。再说一次，他有利益相关。但我认为理查德对整个人类后轨迹的对话有很多贡献，有一些事情我真的很想强调，节目说明中会有更多细节。首先是坦率承认人类现在并不掌控一切。他坚定地强调自然是一个动态系统，而不是一个我们可以坐在山顶上称王称霸的游戏，哪怕只有 5 分钟，更不用说 500 万年了。一切都在我们周围不断变化和移动，我们所能做的最好就是找到在宇宙中存在的最佳方式，而自然已经在尝试这样做，我们最多只能顺应这些轨迹。我们冻结任何东西、冻结我们的形态、冻结我们的价值观都是荒谬的。我完全同意理查德，我认为关于宏大的人类后轨迹，需要消除的一个要素是，我们可以选择的选项之一是永恒的人科王国。我认为这是一个非常荒谬的假设。我认为变化的速度，也许还有变化的方向。如果我们有任何意志，也许我们可以影响它。但我们正在驾驭这些变化的浪潮，理查德对此非常坚定。我的一些类比，比如手握方向盘、交接接力棒，他说得对，实际情况比这更模糊、更狂野。它总是在变化，我真的相信这是一个我会更多地融入我的写作中的视角，并且应该更多地出现在关于人类后未来的非常坦率的对话中。与本系列每一集以及前一系列每一集一样，在节目说明中你会看到一篇完整的文章。在这种情况下，它将列出理查德的“值得的继任者”标准，以及他对创新者和监管者应该如何处理“值得的继任者”动态的想法的概要。在我们即将到来的“值得的继任者”系列第三集中，我们将回到哲学，与一位我认为是过去二十多年来最受欢迎的 AGI 和超人类主义思想家之一的人对话。所以你得等到下一集才能知道我们请到了谁，但我希望你喜欢今天的节目，下次在轨迹中再见。
